title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: stalld bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for stalld is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: The stalld program monitors the set of system threads, looking for threads that are ready-to-run but have not been given processor time for some threshold period. When a starving thread is found, it is given a temporary boost using the SCHED_DEADLINE policy. The default is to allow 10 microseconds of runtime for 1 second of clock time.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): [RHEL-9] Stalld 1.19.8+ doesn't set sched_fifo in systemd unit file [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-109534)

full_text:
An update for stalld is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

The stalld program monitors the set of system threads, looking for threads that are ready-to-run but have not been given processor time for some threshold period. When a starving thread is found, it is given a temporary boost using the SCHED_DEADLINE policy. The default is to allow 10 microseconds of runtime for 1 second of clock time.Bug Fix(es) and Enhancement(s): [RHEL-9] Stalld 1.19.8+ doesn't set sched_fifo in systemd unit file [rhel-9.6.z] (JIRA:RHEL-109534)
